4 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

The biggest misconception about AGI might be the assumption that we’re waiting for it to arrive.
Everyone is asking the wrong question.
When will AGI happen?
Will AGI replace us?
What will AGI think?
But after spending years watching the AI industry evolve at breakneck speed, I’ve become convinced that we may have misunderstood the destination entirely.
What if AGI isn’t a machine becoming human?
What if AGI is humans becoming more than human?
And what if, in some strange way, we’ve already built the first rough prototype?
⸻
The Great AGI Illusion
The popular imagination treats Artificial General Intelligence like some alien species waiting to emerge from a datacenter.
A digital god.
A superhuman mind.
An autonomous intelligence that wakes up one morning and decides humanity is obsolete.
But that’s not what today’s AI systems actually do.
Despite the astonishing progress of large language models and AI agents, they remain, fundamentally, prediction engines. They predict the next token, the next action, the next pattern based on incomprehensibly vast amounts of human-generated data.
That’s extraordinary.
But it’s not independent thought.
AI doesn’t decide what matters.
AI doesn’t choose its own goals.
AI doesn’t discover meaning.
It waits.
For a prompt.
For a direction.
For a human.
And that’s where the conversation becomes interesting.
⸻
AI Is Not Replacing Human Intelligence. It’s Amplifying It.
Consider a simple observation:
The same AI system can produce groundbreaking scientific insights, write a bestselling marketing campaign, create beautiful art, or generate complete nonsense.
What’s the difference?
Not the model.
Not the hardware.
Not the algorithms.
The human driving it.
The uncomfortable truth about AI-generated “slop” flooding the internet isn’t that AI is bad.
It’s that humans are often bad at directing it.
The quality of the output remains tightly coupled to the quality of the human intent behind it.
In this sense, AI may already represent something remarkably close to practical AGI:
Human general intelligence running on vastly better hardware.
⸻
We Already Have a Primitive Version of AGI
This sounds heretical to many researchers.
But think about it.
Take an expert scientist.
Give them access to AI agents capable of processing millions of documents, running simulations, generating hypotheses, and exploring possibilities at machine speed.
What do you get?
Not artificial intelligence replacing human intelligence.
You get augmented human intelligence.
A human who can think, create, and discover at scales that would have appeared supernatural to every previous generation.
To a person living in 1920, a modern human equipped with AI agents would look indistinguishable from an AGI.
Perhaps we’ve been waiting for something that already arrived — just not in the form we expected.
⸻
The Mistake of Treating AGI as a Single Supermind
Many AGI discussions implicitly assume that intelligence scales linearly:
One genius AI.
Then a million copies.
Then unimaginable progress.
But there’s a problem.
As physicist and philosopher David Deutsch has argued, progress doesn’t emerge from duplicating a single mind endlessly.
Progress emerges from many different minds pursuing many different problems.
Economists call this comparative advantage.
A billion copies of the same genius aren’t a civilization.
They’re a bottleneck.
The true breakthrough isn’t cloning intelligence.
It’s amplifying the unique intelligence already distributed among billions of humans.
⸻
Why Human Diversity Matters More Than Artificial Superintelligence
Imagine two futures.
In the first, humanity builds one perfect AGI and replicates it infinitely.
In the second, every human being gains access to tools that increase their cognitive capabilities by orders of magnitude.
Which world produces more discoveries?
More art?
More science?
More meaning?
The answer seems obvious.
The real “country of geniuses” won’t emerge from one artificial supermind.
It will emerge from billions of enhanced human minds.
Each different.
Each curious.
Each pursuing entirely different questions.
⸻
At Curios, This Is Exactly What We Believe
At Curios, we firmly believe that the ultimate tool humanity can build is one that amplifies human curiosity itself.
Curiosity is the engine behind every scientific revolution, every artistic movement, every entrepreneurial breakthrough, and every civilization-level leap forward.
AI doesn’t replace that force.
It fuels it.
It boosts it.
It extends it.
The future isn’t about building machines that think instead of us.
It’s about building tools that allow humans to think beyond what was previously possible.
⸻
Maybe Marshall McLuhan Saw This Coming
Media theorist Marshall McLuhan once suggested that when an extension of humanity becomes sufficiently advanced, we stop noticing it as a tool and simply begin living through it.
Perhaps that’s what AI ultimately becomes.
Not a separate intelligence.
Not an alien mind.
Just us.
Extended.
Amplified.
Expanded beyond what we previously believed possible.
And maybe that’s what AGI was all along:
Not artificial intelligence becoming human.
But human intelligence becoming something more.
⸻
The journey to AGI may not be a giant leap after all.
It may simply be millions of humans taking tiny steps forward, together.
Your turn to walk toward AGI — one snail’s step at a time. 🐌
Visit CuriosAI.com today and ask a truly great question.
The answer might surprise you. 🙋
